km.Padova, Veneto, Italy: ផាដុវ៉ា, វេនែតូ, ប្រទេសអ៊ីតាលី

zh.lifeSummary: Jacopo de Carrara是Gia Comino II de Carrara的兒子，於1207年出生在義大利維圖帕多瓦。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1242年在他的家鄉享年35歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jacopo de Carrara是Gia Comino II de Carrara的儿子，于1207年出生在意大利維圖帕多瓦。他至少有1个儿子。他去世于1242年在他的家乡享年35岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Carrara
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Guido, Angelo, Alessandro, Amerigo, Antonio, Arrigo, Carlo, Enzo, Gaetano, Giovanni, Guerino.
Italian (southern): habitational name from any of various places called Carrara, from Latin carraria ‘quarry’.

nameMeanings.name: Jacopo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jesus, Francisco, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Rafael, Alfredo, Luis, Carlos, Mario, Roberto.
Spanish and Italian: from the Latin personal name Jacobus (see James , Giacomo ).

variants.name: James
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

variants.name: Bacci
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dante, Amedeo, Amelio, Carlo, Enea, Ettore, Giancarlo, Leonardo, Romano, Romeo, Spartaco, Vincenzo.

Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Baccio, a shortened form of various pet names, as for example Jacobaccio (see Jacobo ), Bartolaccio (see Bartolomeo ), and Brunaccio (see Bruno ).

en.lifeSummary: Jacopo de Carrara was born in 1207, in Padova, Veneto, Italy as the son of Gia Comino II de Carrara. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1242, in his hometown, at the age of 35.

fr.lifeSummary: Jacopo de Carrara est né en 1207, à Padova, Veneto, Italie, fils de Gia Comino II de Carrara. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1242, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 35 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Jacopo de Carrara wurde 1207 in Padova, Veneto, Italien, als Sohn von Gia Comino II de Carrara geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1242 im Alter von 35 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Jacopo de Carrara è nato il nel 1207 nel Padova, Veneto, Italia come figlio di Gia Comino II de Carrara. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1242, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 35 anni.

ja.Padova, Veneto, Italy: イタリアベンエトープアドヴァ
ja.lifeSummary: Jacopo de Carraraは1207年にイタリアベンエトープアドヴァでGia Comino II de Carraraの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1242年に故郷で35歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jacopo de Carrara(은)는 1207년 이탈리아 븐토 파도바에서 Gia Comino II de Carrara의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1242년 그의 고향에서 35세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jacopo de Carrara nasceu em 1207, em Padova, Veneto, Itália filho de Gia Comino II de Carrara. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1242, em sua cidade natal, com 35 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Jacopo de Carrara родился в 1207 г. в местности падова, винето, Италия. Его отца звали Gia Comino II de Carrara. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1242 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 35 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Jacopo de Carrara nació en 1207, en Padova, Veneto, Italia como hijo de Gia Comino II de Carrara. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1242, en su pueblo, a la edad de 35 años.

th.Padova, Veneto, Italy: ปาโดแว, เฟเอนเอโทะ, อิตาลี
